_meta.source: The Campaign for North Africa (SPI, 1979) — Scenario Group Five, Case 64.0
notes: The Campaign Game has no unique unit setup — it references Scenario 60 or 61 for initial deployment.
This file captures only the campaign-specific adjustments (replacement pool changes, initiative, special rules).
At runtime, the engine loads the referenced base_scenario's setup, then applies these adjustments.
Designer's note: 'requires not so much a commitment in time... but a commitment to planning and foresight. A campaign game will need at least ten players, perhaps more.' (64.1)

scenario.name: The Campaign for North Africa
scenario.description: The full campaign — the entire North African war from the Italian offensive in September 1940 through the Axis retreat to Tunisia. Two entry points available.

sub_scenarios.name: Full Campaign
sub_scenarios.note: Uses Scenario 60 initial deployment and initiative rules

sub_scenarios.name: Shorter Campaign (Rommel's Arrival)
sub_scenarios.note: Uses Scenario 61 initial deployment and initiative rules

scenario.initiative_note: Use initiative rules from the referenced base scenario (60.0 or 61.0). Normal procedure once base scenario restrictions expire.

notes: No restrictions on normal game actions (Raids on Rommel, Commonwealth Fleet, etc. may all be performed as per rules).
Axis Player's Malta Air Availability uses the campaign game row on the Axis Strategic Airforce Commitment Chart (64.52).

axis_truck_production.note: These are total trucks Axis may bring in for the entire campaign (not per turn)

italian_production_chart.note: One-half of all replacement points plannable through Game-Turn 24 are unavailable
italian_production_chart.exception: Axis Player still has 60 CV L.3 and CV 33/35 tank replacement points available

production_chart_reductions.note: These specific items are REDUCED TO (not by) the listed numbers. All other replacement points unaffected.

air_and_logistics_abstractions.designer_note: 'Why anyone would play a campaign game without the Air and/or Logistics Game(s) is beyond me.' — Designer (64.6)
air_and_logistics_abstractions.instructions: Players pick start point (Italian Offensive or Desert Fox) and use abstraction rules from the appropriate scenario section (32.0, 47.0, or 58.0), plus the replacement point adjustments in Case 64.5.

victory_conditions.designer_note: 'It would be hard to imagine anything more exasperating than playing a game for five years, getting to the end, and finding out that you have lost by one point or two hexes... Victory conditions for a game of this size and scope are almost meaningless: survival itself is a victory.' — Designer (64.7)
victory_conditions.method: Point-based — see PDF pages 88-89 for complete VP table and threshold levels
victory_conditions.vp_location_table: _note: VP table spans pages 88-89 and was not fully captured due to OCR column alignment issues. See source PDF rule 64.71-64.76 for complete conditions.

known_thresholds.note: Full threshold table in PDF page 89
